content:
# Session 16 (iter-016) — recommendations for the next plan iter

## CRITICAL — do NOT re-assign ★'s mechanical closers (STUCK → escalated)
- ★ `tensorObjAssoc_eta_factor_sheaf` (SGR.lean L2737): the math is **DONE** — its residual IS the
  now-proven `tensorObjAssoc_associator_counit_coherence` (axiom-clean). The remaining obstruction is a
  **comp-instance-diamond `isDefEq` on the ~1.2M-char concrete term that needs >4,000,000 heartbeats**.
  This iter independently re-confirmed that `exact`, ordered `erw`, `simp`/`rw`, and `subst`/`conv` ALL
  hit the same term-size+diamond wall ≫ the 200k limit. **Do NOT send another prover lane at ★ with any
  mechanical-closer directive** — it is a verified dead end (4+ iters of the same wall: 013/014/015/016).
- Required corrective is STRUCTURAL, not a tactic tweak. Two candidate routes (neither file-local-mechanical):
  1. **Eliminate the comp-instance diamond at the monoidal-setup source** so ★'s goal `≫` resolves to a
     single `CategoryStruct.comp` head (`LocalizedMonoidal` vs `Scheme.Modules.instCategory`
     reconciliation). Then `exact <coherence>` / `rw` / `simp` become cheap. → candidate for a
     **refactor** subagent or **mathlib-analogist (api-alignment)** on the `Category`/`MonoidalCategory`
     instance the project pins on `X.Modules` vs the localized copy.
  2. A bespoke `@[reducible]`/`abbrev` comp-bridge or `Category`-instance reconciliation making the two
     comp instances **syntactically** equal (not merely `rfl`-defeq), so the `isDefEq` short-circuits.
- This is a user/strategy decision (surfaced in TO_USER.md). Until a structural fix lands, ★ stays `sorry`.

## Closest-to-bankable (assign these instead of ★)
- **B6 `tensorPowAdd_assoc`** (L3005, sorries L3032/L3040): **diamond-free pentagon, independent of ★.**
  iter-014 already reduced the base case to the explicit unit-triangle coherence; succ leg →
  `MonoidalCategory.pentagon`. This is the highest-value lane that does NOT touch the diamond wall — it
  can make real sorry-count progress while ★ waits for the structural decision.
- Note B7 `sectionsMul_mul_assoc` (L3045) is gated on ★→B4→B5 + B6 + the μ-slide; do NOT assign until ★
  closes. The 3 comm decls (`tensorBraiding_self_eq_id_of_isInvertible` L3082, `tensorPowAdd_comm` L3096,
  `sectionsMul_mul_comm` L3117) are `[IsInvertible L]`-gated FUTURE work with no consumer — leave parked.

## Reusable proof patterns discovered this iter (bank in Knowledge Base)
- **Stage-across-declarations for heavy abstract monoidal goals.** When a single `simp`/`isDefEq`/
  `kabstract` over the goal exceeds 200k heartbeats, split the proof into a `\uses`-linked chain of tiny
  private lemmas, each doing ONE simp pass + ONE handoff `exact`. Generalising the goal to a uniform
  `[MonoidalCategory M]` (stripping the concrete diamond) is what makes the pieces tractable.
- **Replace `subst h` with `congrArg`/term-mode** when `h`'s `kabstract` motive over a large goal blows
  the budget. `subst` is heartbeat-pathological on big monoidal terms.
- The associator-naturality coherence closer set: `whisker_exchange_assoc`,
  `associator_naturality_{left,middle,right}_assoc`, `comp_whiskerRight`, `whiskerLeft_comp`,
  `hom_inv_whiskerRight_assoc`, `inv_hom_whiskerRight_assoc`, `Iso.hom_inv_id(_assoc)`.

## Coverage debt (1-to-1, for the planner to blueprint)
- `archon dag-query unmatched` = **331** (was 324; +7). The 7 NEW are the private staging lemmas this
  iter (all in `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules`, SGR.lean), depending only on the Mathlib monoidal
  simp set listed above:
  `neF_prefix_id`, `counit_assoc_tail_id`, `tensorObjAssoc_associator_counit_coherence_tail`,
  `tensorObjAssoc_associator_counit_coherence_stage0/1/2`, `tensorObjAssoc_associator_counit_coherence`.
  They are `private` mechanical implementation details of ★ — the planner may either blueprint
  `lem:tensorObjAssoc_eta_factor_sheaf`'s proof to `\uses` a single new
  `lem:tensorObjAssoc_associator_counit_coherence` atom (cleanest), or leave them as un-blueprinted
  helper bricks (like the `RelativeTensorCoequalizer` bricks). Bulk 324 = inherited out-of-cone artifacts.

## Blueprint follow-ups (from lean-vs-blueprint-checker, non-must-fix)
- **Dangling `\lean{}` hints** (3): `sectionGradedRing_gsemiring`, `sectionGradedRing_gcommSemiring`,
  `sectionGradedModule_gmodule` name decls that don't exist in SGR.lean. I added `% NOTE:` annotations;
  the durable fix is for the planner to scaffold sorry-bodied instance/def stubs (so the statement-`\leanok`
  machinery becomes meaningful) — these keep tripping blueprint-doctor/sync otherwise.

## Stale `.lean` comments (from lean-auditor — review can't edit `.lean`; flag for a prover/refactor touch)
- SGR.lean L731–783: comment calls `relTensorActL/ActR/Proj` "DEFERRED/BLOCKED" but they are fully proved
  just above. Stale; correct on the next prover touch of the file.
- `RelativeSpec.lean` header: says "5 sorry bodies" when all 6 pinned declarations now have genuine proofs.

## Tooling (recurring) — surface to user, not auto-fixable in-loop
- `sync_leanok` over-strips `Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex` every iter (3rd consecutive: 014/015/016
  removed 19/21/21 axiom-clean proof-block `\leanok`). Review restores them each time → pure churn, and
  the DAG under-reports SGR between sync and review. Durable fix is sync-side (raise per-decl build budget
  on this large/slow file, or skip re-stripping already-clean decls). In TO_USER.md.
